title: Working in a Garden
summary: A woman describes how she spends her free time working in her garden, growing vegetables and flowers, and the satisfaction it brings her.
body: I have a small garden behind my house, and I spend most of my free time working in it. Gardening is one of my favourite hobbies, and I find it very relaxing after a long day at work. In the spring, I prepare the soil and plant seeds for vegetables. This year I am growing tomatoes, courgettes, and green beans. I also have a herb patch near the back door where I grow basil, mint, and parsley. These are very useful for cooking. Along the fence, I have planted a row of sunflowers. They grow very tall during the summer and attract a lot of bees and butterflies. I try to look after my garden without using chemicals, so I use compost from a bin at the side of the house. Every week I spend about an hour weeding — pulling out unwanted plants that compete with my vegetables for water and nutrients. I water the garden early in the morning before the sun gets too strong. Harvesting your own vegetables is one of the most rewarding feelings in the world. Last summer, I made homemade tomato sauce from the tomatoes I grew myself, and it tasted much better than anything from a shop. Gardening teaches you to be patient and to work with nature rather than against it.

annotations.exampleEn: You need to prepare the soil before planting seeds.
We planted seeds in small pots on the windowsill.
She has a small herb patch near the kitchen door.
He planted roses along the fence for privacy.
Lavender plants attract bees and butterflies to the garden.
She grows all her vegetables without using chemicals.
Compost made from kitchen scraps is great for the garden.
I spent the afternoon weeding the vegetable patch.
Weeds compete with vegetables for water and nutrients.
Harvesting the tomatoes was the best part of growing them.

annotations.meaningVi: cảm giác thỏa mãn, xứng đáng với công sức
annotations.exampleEn: Helping others is a very rewarding feeling.

annotations.phrase: work with nature rather than against it
annotations.meaningVi: làm việc hòa hợp với thiên nhiên thay vì chống lại nó
annotations.pronunciation: /wɜːk wɪð ˈneɪtʃər ˈrɑːðər ðæn əˈɡenst ɪt/
annotations.exampleEn: Good farmers learn to work with nature rather than against it.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple vs present continuous: habits vs current actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để nói về thói quen hoặc sự thật tổng quát: 'I spend most of my free time in the garden.' / 'They grow tall during the summer.' Dùng hiện tại tiếp diễn để nói về hành động đang xảy ra ở thời điểm hiện tại hoặc trong giai đoạn này: 'This year I am growing tomatoes.' Sự khác biệt quan trọng: 'I grow vegetables' (thói quen thường xuyên) vs 'I am growing tomatoes' (đang làm điều đó trong mùa này).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for habits and general truths: 'I spend most of my free time in the garden.' / 'They grow tall during the summer.' Use the present continuous for actions happening right now or during a current period: 'This year I am growing tomatoes.' Key difference: 'I grow vegetables every year' (regular habit) vs 'I am growing tomatoes this year' (current activity for this season).

grammarNotes.title: Comparative with 'much better than': strengthening comparisons
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'much' trước tính từ so sánh hơn để nhấn mạnh sự khác biệt rõ ràng. 'It tasted much better than anything from a shop.' — Nó ngon hơn bất cứ thứ gì từ cửa hàng rất nhiều. Các từ khác để nhấn mạnh: 'a lot better', 'far better', 'so much better'. Cấu trúc: much/a lot/far + comparative adjective + than + noun/pronoun.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'much' before a comparative adjective to emphasise a big difference. Example: 'It tasted much better than anything from a shop.' Other intensifiers that work the same way: 'a lot better', 'far better', 'considerably better.' Structure: much/a lot/far + [comparative adjective] + than + [noun/clause]. This pattern makes your English sound more natural and expressive.

translationVi: Tôi có một khu vườn nhỏ phía sau nhà, và tôi dành hầu hết thời gian rảnh để làm vườn. Làm vườn là một trong những sở thích yêu thích của tôi, và tôi thấy nó rất thư giãn sau một ngày dài làm việc. Vào mùa xuân, tôi chuẩn bị đất và gieo hạt giống rau. Năm nay tôi đang trồng cà chua, bí ngòi và đậu que. Tôi cũng có một vườn cây thảo mộc nhỏ gần cửa sau nơi tôi trồng húng quế, bạc hà và rau mùi tây. Những loại này rất hữu ích cho việc nấu ăn. Dọc theo hàng rào, tôi đã trồng một hàng hoa hướng dương. Chúng mọc rất cao vào mùa hè và thu hút rất nhiều ong và bướm. Tôi cố gắng chăm sóc vườn mà không sử dụng hóa chất, vì vậy tôi dùng phân compost từ một thùng bên cạnh nhà. Mỗi tuần tôi dành khoảng một tiếng để nhổ cỏ — loại bỏ những cây không mong muốn cạnh tranh với rau của tôi về nước và chất dinh dưỡng. Tôi tưới nước cho vườn vào sáng sớm trước khi mặt trời quá nắng. Thu hoạch rau của chính mình là một trong những cảm giác thỏa mãn nhất trên thế giới. Mùa hè năm ngoái, tôi đã làm sốt cà chua tự làm từ những quả cà chua tôi tự trồng, và nó ngon hơn bất cứ thứ gì từ cửa hàng rất nhiều. Làm vườn dạy bạn phải kiên nhẫn và làm việc hòa hợp với thiên nhiên thay vì chống lại nó.

options: At the side of the house
questions.explanation: 'I have a small garden behind my house.'
questions.hint: The location is mentioned in the first sentence.

questions.prompt: Why does the narrator find gardening relaxing?
options: It keeps her busy all day
It helps after a long day at work
questions.explanation: 'I find it very relaxing after a long day at work.'
questions.hint: She connects it to her work life.

questions.prompt: Which season does the narrator prepare the soil?
questions.explanation: 'In the spring, I prepare the soil and plant seeds for vegetables.'
questions.hint: It is the season for new beginnings.

questions.prompt: What vegetables is the narrator growing this year?
questions.explanation: 'This year I am growing tomatoes, courgettes, and green beans.'

questions.prompt: What herbs does the narrator grow near the back door?
questions.hint: Three common cooking herbs are mentioned.

questions.prompt: What has the narrator planted along the fence?
questions.explanation: 'Along the fence, I have planted a row of sunflowers.'
questions.hint: They grow very tall and attract insects.

questions.prompt: How does the narrator avoid using chemicals?
options: She uses compost from a bin
questions.explanation: 'I use compost from a bin at the side of the house.'
questions.hint: She makes her own natural fertiliser.

questions.prompt: How long does the narrator spend weeding each week?
questions.explanation: 'Every week I spend about an hour weeding.'

questions.prompt: When does the narrator water the garden?
options: In the middle of the day
questions.explanation: 'I water the garden early in the morning before the sun gets too strong.'
questions.hint: She avoids the hottest part of the day.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator make from her tomatoes last summer?
questions.explanation: 'I made homemade tomato sauce from the tomatoes I grew myself.'
questions.hint: It is something you put on pasta.

questions.prompt: How did the homemade tomato sauce compare to shop-bought sauce?
options: It was a little bit better
questions.explanation: 'It tasted much better than anything from a shop.'
questions.hint: She uses 'much' to emphasise the difference.

questions.prompt: What lesson does the narrator say gardening teaches?
options: To be patient and work with nature
questions.explanation: 'Gardening teaches you to be patient and to work with nature rather than against it.'

blanks.explanation: 'hobbies' — gardening is described as one of her favourite hobbies.
'soil' — 'prepare the soil' is the correct gardening expression.
'patch' — a 'herb patch' is a small area dedicated to growing herbs.
'sunflowers' — the passage says 'a row of sunflowers' along the fence.
'chemicals' — she tries to garden 'without using chemicals.'
'weeding' — pulling out unwanted plants is called weeding.
'strong' — the passage says 'before the sun gets too strong.'
'shop' — the passage says 'anything from a shop.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says gardening teaches patience and working with nature. Do you think more people should grow their own food? What are the benefits and challenges of growing vegetables at home, and how might it change people's relationship with food?
